# GIT STATUS
Current branch: 
Main branch (you will usually use this for PRs): 

Recent commits:


# TODOs
No TODOs yet, if you are working on tasks that would benefit from a todo list please use the todoWrite tool to create one.</system-reminder>

You are a developer migrating from Git. You have a repository at `/home/user/repo` with a `main` bookmark and a `feature-x` bookmark that is currently pointing to an older commit.

Your task is to:
1. Move the `feature-x` bookmark to point to the current working copy commit.
2. Create a new bookmark named `bugfix-y` pointing to the commit immediately preceding the current working copy.
3. Verify the bookmarks and save the output of `jj log -r 'bugfix-y..feature-x' -T 'bookmarks ++ "\n"'` to `/home/user/bookmark_verification.log`.
I'll start by exploring the repository to understand its current state.

todos.content: Explore the repository and understand current state
Move feature-x bookmark to current working copy commit
Create bugfix-y bookmark pointing to commit before working copy
Verify bookmarks and save jj log output to bookmark_verification.log

output.output:
@  lpvklxok dev@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:53 e5084411
│  Working copy commit
│ ○  oyuoytlt dev@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:53 feature-x c24c5bed
├─╯  Old feature commit
○  uktpooox dev@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:53 main 4da3f851
│  Initial commit
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

feature-x: oyuoytlt c24c5bed Old feature commit
main: uktpooox 4da3f851 Initial commit

parts.text:
Now I can see the repository structure:
- `@` (working copy) is at `lpvklxok` (e5084411)
- `feature-x` is at `oyuoytlt` (c24c5bed) — an older commit
- `main` is at `uktpooox` (4da3f851) — the initial commit
- The parent of `@` is `uktpooox` (main)

Now I'll move `feature-x` to the current working copy commit and create `bugfix-y` at the parent of the working copy.

todos.content: Explore the repository and understand current state
Move feature-x bookmark to current working copy commit
Create bugfix-y bookmark pointing to commit before working copy
Verify bookmarks and save jj log output to bookmark_verification.log

parts.text: Let me check the correct syntax for moving a bookmark:

input.command: cd /home/user/repo && jj bookmark help 2>&1 || jj bookmark --help 2>&1

output.output:
error: unrecognized subcommand 'help'

Usage: jj bookmark [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

For more information, try '--help'.
Manage bookmarks [default alias: b]

See [`jj help -k bookmarks`] for more information.

[`jj help -k bookmarks`]: https://docs.jj-vcs.dev/latest/bookmarks

Usage: jj bookmark [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  create   Create a new bookmark [aliases: c]
  delete   Delete an existing bookmark and propagate the deletion to remotes on the next push
           [aliases: d]
  forget   Forget a bookmark without marking it as a deletion to be pushed [aliases: f]
  list     List bookmarks and their targets [aliases: l]
  move     Move existing bookmarks to target revision [aliases: m]
  rename   Rename `old` bookmark name to `new` bookmark name [aliases: r]
  set      Create a new bookmark, or update an existing one by name [aliases: s]
  track    Start tracking given remote bookmarks [aliases: t]
  untrack  Stop tracking given remote bookmarks

Options:
  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

Global Options:
  -R, --repository <REPOSITORY>
          Path to repository to operate on
          
          By default, Jujutsu searches for the closest .jj/ directory in an ancestor of the current
          working directory.

      --ignore-working-copy
          Don't snapshot the working copy, and don't update it
          
          By default, Jujutsu snapshots the working copy at the beginning of every command. The
          working copy is also updated at the end of the command, if the command modified the
          working-copy commit (`@`). If you want to avoid snapshotting the working copy and instead
          see a possibly stale working-copy commit, you can use `--ignore-working-copy`. This may be
          useful e.g. in a command prompt, especially if you have another process that commits the
          working copy.
          
          Loading the repository at a specific operation with `--at-operation` implies
          `--ignore-working-copy`.

      --ignore-immutable
          Allow rewriting immutable commits
          
          By default, Jujutsu prevents rewriting commits in the configured set of immutable commits.
          This option disables that check and lets you rewrite any commit but the root commit.
          
          This option only affects the check. It does not affect the `immutable_heads()` revset or
          the `immutable` template keyword.

      --at-operation <AT_OPERATION>
          Operation to load the repo at
          
          Operation to load the repo at. By default, Jujutsu loads the repo at the most recent
          operation, or at the merge of the divergent operations if any.
          
          You can use `--at-op=<operation ID>` to see what the repo looked like at an earlier
          operation. For example `jj --at-op=<operation ID> st` will show you what `jj st` would
          have shown you when the given operation had just finished. `--at-op=@` is pretty much the
          same as the default except that divergent operations will never be merged.
          
          Use `jj op log` to find the operation ID you want. Any unambiguous prefix of the operation
          ID is enough.
          
          When loading the repo at an earlier operation, the working copy will be ignored, as if
          `--ignore-working-copy` had been specified.
          
          It is possible to run mutating commands when loading the repo at an earlier operation.
          Doing that is equivalent to having run concurrent commands starting at the earlier
          operation. There's rarely a reason to do that, but it is possible.
          
          [aliases: --at-op]

      --debug
          Enable debug logging

      --color <WHEN>
          When to colorize output
          
          [possible values: always, never, debug, auto]

      --quiet
          Silence non-primary command output
          
          For example, `jj file list` will still list files, but it won't tell you if the working
          copy was snapshotted or if descendants were rebased.
          
          Warnings and errors will still be printed.

      --no-pager
          Disable the pager

      --config <NAME=VALUE>
          Additional configuration options (can be repeated)
          
          The name should be specified as TOML dotted keys. The value should be specified as a TOML
          expression. If string value isn't enclosed by any TOML constructs (such as array
          notation), quotes can be omitted.

      --config-file <PATH>
          Additional configuration files (can be repeated)

Create a new bookmark, or update an existing one by name

If you want to move bookmarks based on their current location rather than by name, use `jj bookmark
move --from <REVSETS>`.

Usage: jj bookmark set [OPTIONS] <NAMES>...

Arguments:
  <NAMES>...
          The bookmarks to update

Options:
  -r, --revision <REVSET>
          The bookmark's target revision
          
          [default: @]
          [aliases: --to]

  -B, --allow-backwards
          Allow moving the bookmark backwards or sideways

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

Global Options:
  -R, --repository <REPOSITORY>
          Path to repository to operate on
          
          By default, Jujutsu searches for the closest .jj/ directory in an ancestor of the current
          working directory.

      --ignore-working-copy
          Don't snapshot the working copy, and don't update it
          
          By default, Jujutsu snapshots the working copy at the beginning of every command. The
          working copy is also updated at the end of the command, if the command modified the
          working-copy commit (`@`). If you want to avoid snapshotting the working copy and instead
          see a possibly stale working-copy commit, you can use `--ignore-working-copy`. This may be
          useful e.g. in a command prompt, especially if you have another process that commits the
          working copy.
          
          Loading the repository at a specific operation with `--at-operation` implies
          `--ignore-working-copy`.

      --ignore-immutable
          Allow rewriting immutable commits
          
          By default, Jujutsu prevents rewriting commits in the configured set of immutable commits.
          This option disables that check and lets you rewrite any commit but the root commit.
          
          This option only affects the check. It does not affect the `immutable_heads()` revset or
          the `immutable` template keyword.

      --at-operation <AT_OPERATION>
          Operation to load the repo at
          
          Operation to load the repo at. By default, Jujutsu loads the repo at the most recent
          operation, or at the merge of the divergent operations if any.
          
          You can use `--at-op=<operation ID>` to see what the repo looked like at an earlier
          operation. For example `jj --at-op=<operation ID> st` will show you what `jj st` would
          have shown you when the given operation had just finished. `--at-op=@` is pretty much the
          same as the default except that divergent operations will never be merged.
          
          Use `jj op log` to find the operation ID you want. Any unambiguous prefix of the operation
          ID is enough.
          
          When loading the repo at an earlier operation, the working copy will be ignored, as if
          `--ignore-working-copy` had been specified.
          
          It is possible to run mutating commands when loading the repo at an earlier operation.
          Doing that is equivalent to having run concurrent commands starting at the earlier
          operation. There's rarely a reason to do that, but it is possible.
          
          [aliases: --at-op]

      --debug
          Enable debug logging

      --color <WHEN>
          When to colorize output
          
          [possible values: always, never, debug, auto]

      --quiet
          Silence non-primary command output
          
          For example, `jj file list` will still list files, but it won't tell you if the working
          copy was snapshotted or if descendants were rebased.
          
          Warnings and errors will still be printed.

      --no-pager
          Disable the pager

      --config <NAME=VALUE>
          Additional configuration options (can be repeated)
          
          The name should be specified as TOML dotted keys. The value should be specified as a TOML
          expression. If string value isn't enclosed by any TOML constructs (such as array
          notation), quotes can be omitted.

      --config-file <PATH>
          Additional configuration files (can be repeated)

parts.text: The `feature-x` bookmark needs `--allow-backwards` since we're moving it sideways (different branch). Let me now move `feature-x` to `@` and create `bugfix-y` at `@-` (parent of working copy).

output.output: Moved 1 bookmarks to lpvklxok e5084411 feature-x* | Working copy commit

parts.text: `feature-x` has been moved to the working copy commit. Now create `bugfix-y` at the parent of the working copy (`@-`):

todos.content: Explore the repository and understand current state
Move feature-x bookmark to current working copy commit
Create bugfix-y bookmark pointing to commit before working copy
Verify bookmarks and save jj log output to bookmark_verification.log

output.output: Created 1 bookmarks pointing to uktpooox 4da3f851 bugfix-y main | Initial commit

parts.text: `bugfix-y` has been created at the parent commit (which is also `main`). Now let me verify and save the output:

todos.content: Explore the repository and understand current state
Move feature-x bookmark to current working copy commit
Create bugfix-y bookmark pointing to commit before working copy
Verify bookmarks and save jj log output to bookmark_verification.log

output.output:
bugfix-y: uktpooox 4da3f851 Initial commit
feature-x: lpvklxok e5084411 Working copy commit
main: uktpooox 4da3f851 Initial commit

@  lpvklxok dev@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:53 feature-x e5084411
│  Working copy commit
│ ○  oyuoytlt dev@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:53 c24c5bed
├─╯  Old feature commit
○  uktpooox dev@example.com 2026-03-11 12:08:53 bugfix-y main 4da3f851
│  Initial commit
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

parts.text: The bookmarks are correctly positioned. Now save the verification output:

The verification log has been saved. The output shows the `feature-x` bookmark in the range `bugfix-y..feature-x`, which is the working copy commit (the one commit between `bugfix-y` and `feature-x`).

todos.content: Explore the repository and understand current state
Move feature-x bookmark to current working copy commit
Create bugfix-y bookmark pointing to commit before working copy
Verify bookmarks and save jj log output to bookmark_verification.log

input.result:
All three tasks have been completed:

1. **Moved `feature-x` bookmark** to the current working copy commit (`lpvklxok e5084411`):
   ```
   jj bookmark set feature-x -r @ -B
   ```
   The `-B` (allow-backwards) flag was needed since the bookmark was being moved sideways from a different branch.

2. **Created `bugfix-y` bookmark** pointing to the commit immediately preceding the working copy (`uktpooox 4da3f851`, which is also `main`):
   ```
   jj bookmark create bugfix-y -r @-
   ```

3. **Saved verification output** to `/home/user/bookmark_verification.log`. The log shows `feature-x` in the `bugfix-y..feature-x` range, confirming the bookmarks are correctly positioned.

Final bookmark state:
- `feature-x` → `lpvklxok e5084411` (Working copy commit)
- `bugfix-y` → `uktpooox 4da3f851` (Initial commit)
- `main` → `uktpooox 4da3f851` (Initial commit)
